video.title: Tour Of Flanders 2017 – GCN's Top 11 Riders To Watch

all_candidates.title: Tour Of Flanders 2017 – GCN's Top 11 Riders To Watch
GCN's Tour Of Flanders 2017 Preview Show

transcript.clean_text: At 259 and a half kilometers, the 101st edition of the men's rondan blandin or tour of Flanders features 18 of the famous Flemish Bergs or Helingan, including the welcome return of the Mu Van Herdsburgen. That however comes much sooner in the race than it used to and the last 75 km remain the same as last year's event. The women's is up to 153 km, so 12 more than last year where we saw Lizzie Dagen take her first win at the event in a very close fought battle with Emma Johansson. Both events take place on Sunday the 2nd of April 2017. And coming up are 11 riders we think you should watch out for. And we've got to start with the winners from 2016. Peter Sagan and Lizzie Armitstead who were both world champions at the time and therefore the perfect victors really for what was the 100th edition of the tour of Fllanders. This year Sagan returned in his rainbow band with three wins already under his belt in 2017 including Kuna Brussels Kerna and a close second place finish to Mihal Kikovski in the only other monument so far Milan Sanmo. This year in 2017 he has so far done 18 road races and of those he's finished on the podium 11 times. So you'd expect him to at least podium at this year's Tour of Flanders. Diagonal on the other hand has had a much quieter start to the year. She's only done two days of racing so far. In fact, her best placing being a third at Straa Biana and event she won the previous year. And in fact, she didn't race against Wum last Sunday due to illness. So a repeat win for her is very much in question. At the other end of the scale, we have Lepisto of Sevelilia who is on fire. She's just won dward of Landren and Gentum in the space of a few days. And although she's only competed at the Tour of Flanders twice, her best placing being 28, both she and her team are going to be full of confidence this time round. Of the monuments that John Denb is capable of winning, the tour of Fllanders is the only one he is yet to win. In fact, his best placing was seventh a couple of years ago. Last year, the classics were a writeoff for the German after horrendous crash at training in Calpe in Spain. But he's back towards his best this year. He didn't quite have what it took to follow Peter Sagan's attack on the Podger Milan San Roma, but arguably the short sharp BS of flyers are better suited to him than the longer climbs down in Italy. And his new team Trex Sega Freda are certainly not short of experience. They've won this race a couple of times before with Fabian Canelara, so he's certainly got a strong team to back him up. Alexander Kristoff of Kushia has not finished outside the top four of this race for the past four years. In fact, he won it back in 2015. Now, like Don Cobb, he was unable to follow Peter Scan's attack at Milan San Ramo. However, he did win the bunch sprint for fourth behind. And so, he's obviously a man on decent form. An untimely puncture took him out of the equation at the recent Gent Webergum. And although his team's not as strong as some out there, he'll still be confident of a top result here. It's hard to believe that BMC's Greg Vanovar has yet to win one of cycling's five big monuments. He's won the omniplad twice. He's just won Gent Waggum. He's the Olympic champion and he was also the yellow jersey at the Tour to France last year after winning a stage. However, he is not going to be satisfied until he wins one of cycling's biggest one-day races, and he's best suited to the Tour of Flanders. So, he's probably Belgian's best hope of ending a 4-year dry spell for the country at their home race. Tom Bonan has won this race on three occasions previously. However, he's not performed quite so well since the organizers changed the route back in 2013. His explosive power perhaps isn't quite what it used to be up the short steep climbs. But what he does have going for him is the fact that quickstep floors his team are probably the strongest in the race with multiple cars to play. On the other hand though, he's probably going to be the most marked man out of the team. And you do get the impression he's focused slightly more on Parubet the week after than he is on his home race. Set van looks like he could be forever the nearly man of the Cobble Classics. He's finished in the top four on five occasions at either the tour of Flanders or Paru Bay, but has yet to cross the line first. In 2016, he was the last man to be able to stick with Peter Sagan, but ultimately got dropped just on the Patterburg. And this year, he's back with Canondelle Dra. His best result being a third place at the Loop Heet News Blad. However, like Lizzie Dagnan, he didn't start gent due to illness, so his form is in doubt. Winner here two years ago, Alisa Longo Bulgini of Wiggle High Five has been incredibly consistent over the years at the Tour of Flanders. In fact, fifth place last year was her worst result since 2012. She started off this season with a bang, taking the first round of the women's world tour, the Straa Biana, in some style, and she's definitely one to watch this time around. Runner up to Alisa Longo Bulgini back in 2015 was teammate Yolin Dora. And once again they will form a formidable duo in 2017. Dura started off the season fantastically well taking a win in the omloot vanet hagalan and she came agonizingly close to winning gate welver too. And as the current Belgian national champion she's going to be more motivated than ever to perform well on home roads. And we shall finish with yet another former winner of this race from back in 2014 Ellen Van Djk. This year she is racing for team Sunweb and she was pivotal in Corin Rivera's recent win at the Trafa Alfredo Binda. The 30-year-old is as strong as an ox, so she gets a small gap. The rest of the field may never see her again. So, those are our 11 riders to watch in 2017. You can let us know who your favorites are and our glaring emissions as per usual in the comments section down below. And coming up now are two more videos that we think you might be interested in coming up to the tour of Fllanders. Before you go to them, make sure you subscribe by clicking on the globe which you can see on the screen right now. And then for our full tour of Flanders preview, you can click on the video just down there. Or for some advice on how best to ride the cobblestone, you can click just down there.
